fault_hypothesis: The expiry condition on line 66 uses strict greater-than (`now > effective_deadline`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= effective_deadline`), so an offer checked at exactly the deadline is incorrectly treated as still valid.

constraints.intent: When now equals the effective_deadline the condition should trigger expiry (now >= deadline), but the strict > misses this case.
After computing effective_deadline, verify that an offer at exactly that timestamp will be caught by the expiry check (now >= effective_deadline).
In the else branch (offer is valid), now must be strictly less than effective_deadline; equality should have triggered expiry.
Ensure effective_deadline is a numeric type so that the comparison operator behaves correctly at the boundary.
